74168.subject: Well Informed: Can you get rid of your wrinkles?
74168.body:
<link rel=stylesheet href=https://marber-cdn.economist.com/foundations/latest/css/font-face.css>
 The Economist

 
 


 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MDB9AAcAAAAAAmP6qPTkdR3DFd9_0MUspDEyci648zfQIJloDtCsLCopYdRkvMw1_mvx4t6y9TM7HKPTTEsQmdp0M8WW3zX4EwtHDY1TtWWVoMLrdr9K3lkJ 

June 7th 2026

 

 
 
 
Well Informed

 Your evidence-based guide to health and wellness


 
 
 






 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MDB9AAcAAAAAAmP6qPTldSeHQvU4U0z8x_wSrRv4hXu87LUoxm3ODqahA44PIDSfWYImQ_hfejq0-nM_-fnKVAvT0HkN3jfiYMvnvHivxoADwTafhpos-af1 

https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MDB9AAcAAAAAAmP6qPTmHVKplS79r5ih-yaQlnEexj8bFC2UEzKg0X_J-4h-1ByyldWh6DQznXVLXCBcYJJvRo5nCArV-I0zHwtpaUn4-IP9b6OVyxPSEsLo 


Can you get rid of your wrinkles?


 Tim Cross

Senior science writer


 Walk into a chemist’s and you’ll be confronted by shelves full of skin creams boasting of their anti-ageing powers. Purists and pedants (not that I know any) will object that they cannot work by definition: unless physics is badly wrong about many quite fundamental things, it is hard to see how a tub of cream could halt the flow of time.


 But can they at least undo some of the symptoms of ageing, or help reverse the wear and tear that comes with ordinary life? The short answer is that, although many of the claims are based on nothing more solid than consumer surveys or celebrity endorsements, some of the ingredients in face creams 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MDB9A

74163.subject: Your next broker is an AI chatbot 🤑🤖; Catena Labs wants a bank charter for AI Agents, and the OCC said “We’ll Listen” 🏦🤖
74163.body:
Weekly FinTech Digest
